categories.title: GREEK & ROMAN MYTHOLOGY
categories.theme: Facts whose source questions or answers directly reference Greek or Roman mythology figures, places, or legends

clues.clue: In Greek mythology, this hero led the Argonauts on a quest to retrieve the Golden Fleece.
clues.fit_justification: Source question literally reads 'Who in Greek mythology, who led the Argonauts in search of the Golden Fleece?' — directly references Greek mythology.

clues.clue: According to Arthurian legend, this knight conducted a notorious affair with Queen Guinevere, wife of King Arthur.
clues.fit_justification: Source question explicitly states 'According to Arthurian legend' and asks about the knight who had an affair with Queen Guinevere.

clues.clue: This Roman Emperor, whose name is also a musical key, is said to have fiddled while Rome burned.
clues.fit_justification: Source question reads 'Which Roman Emperor is said to have fiddled while Rome burned?' — references a legendary Roman historical myth.

clues.clue: In Greek mythology, this giant bronze automaton served as the protector of the island of Crete.
clues.fit_justification: Source question reads 'Talos, the mythical giant bronze man, was the protector of which island?' — source explicitly labels Talos as mythical and names the island Crete in the answer.

clues.clue: This long narrative poem form, whose name comes from the Greek for 'word' or 'song,' tells the deeds and adventures of a legendary hero.
clues.fit_justification: Source question reads 'What is the name of the long narrative poem that tells of the deeds and adventures of a hero?' — directly describes the mythological literary form; answer is 'Epic'.

finals.title: MYTHOLOGY FINAL
finals.clue: This Roman wine god's ecstatic cult was so disruptive that the Roman Senate moved to suppress it in the 180s BC — the same deity the Greeks called Dionysus.
finals.fit_justification: Source question literally reads 'Which wine god's orgiastic worship was suppressed by the Roman Senate in the 180s BC?' — directly references Roman mythology and names the deity in the answer.

finals.title: MYTHOLOGY FINAL
finals.clue: This Stoic philosopher, once a slave, famously declared that freedom comes not from fulfilling one's desires but from their removal — a teaching echoed in many ancient spiritual traditions.
finals.fit_justification: Source question states 'Which philosopher famously said "Freedom is secured not by the fulfilling of one's desires, but by the removal of desire"?' with answer 'Epictetus' — Epictetus is a figure rooted in Greco-Roman philosophical tradition closely intertwined with ancient mythology and religion.

finals.title: MYTHOLOGY FINAL
finals.clue: In Greek mythology, this island was guarded by the bronze giant Talos — and it is also the birthplace of Zeus according to ancient legend.
finals.fit_justification: Source question reads 'Talos, the mythical giant bronze man, was the protector of which island?' with answer 'Crete' — a mythological fact about a specific island with layered legendary significance.

finals.title: MYTHOLOGY FINAL
finals.clue: In Egyptian mythology, this two-letter word names the life force or spiritual double that every living person was believed to possess.
finals.fit_justification: Source question literally says 'In Egyptian mythology, what is the life force called?' and source answer is 'Ka'.

finals.title: GREEK MYTHOLOGY IN ART
finals.clue: This Greek sculptor, commissioned to create the massive gold-and-ivory statue of Athena inside the Parthenon, is considered one of the greatest artists of the ancient world.
finals.fit_justification: Source question explicitly names 'Greek sculptor', 'Athena Parthenos', and 'Parthenon', all core elements of Greek mythology and religious art; source answer is 'Phidias'.
